_comment: Platform/deployment constants for ai-transport, owned by Platform Governance (Vlad) per ADR-022 §10 Policy Ownership. No Process may modify its own Policy. Introduced by ADR-030 (Controlled Full-Mesh Transport Access).
max_hops_rationale: ADR-030 §3.2: deliberately NOT derived from current participant count (5). Independent platform constant, tunable without redefining loop-protection every time Orchestra gains or loses a participant.

circuit_breaker.on_trigger: Suspend all new writes platform-wide, notify Vlad. Provisional values (ADR-030 §3.5) — not yet tuned against real usage.
circuit_breaker.enforcement_note: No automated monitor currently reads this file. Enforcement requires either a GitHub Action or an n8n workflow watching write volume; neither exists yet. Until one does, this section is a declared intent, not a live control.

dead_letter_reasons.SCHEMA_INVALID: write failed schemas/mission.schema.json validation at commit time (ADR-030 §3.4)

participants_with_physical_access.Grok: GitHub App (scoped to this repo) - LIVE since 2026-07-01
participants_with_physical_access.ChatGPT: STRUCTURALLY LIMITED - native connector is READ-ONLY (write requires Codex, a separate product). See RESEARCH_Model-GitHub-Connectivity_2026-07-01.md.
participants_with_physical_access.Gemini: NOT APPLICABLE BY ROLE, not a gap - Gemini is Workspace Integrator (AI_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY.md), not a transport Mission executor. Its access path is Google Drive -> the Base (read-only per ADR-003 Single Writer Principle, regardless of Drive auth status), a separate system from ai-transport. Decided 2026-07-01 (Vlad): do not pursue ai-transport write access for Gemini.
participants_with_physical_access.Perplexity: NOT AVAILABLE on current (Free) tier - GitHub connector is a Pro/Max/Enterprise feature, absent from Connectors settings on Free. See RESEARCH_Model-GitHub-Connectivity_2026-07-01.md.

access_note: Revised 2026-07-01. Of the original ADR-030 full-mesh assumption: Grok - LIVE (GitHub App). Gemini - reclassified as not applicable, its role doesn't require transport write access; the Base-via-Drive question is separate and unaffected by this. ChatGPT - structurally read-only in its native connector; write would require adopting Codex as a separate workflow, not decided. Perplexity - tier-gated, would need a paid upgrade to even test; exact repo-scoping still unconfirmed in public docs.
